tagging_mechanism: Mechanism (ii) — inspector-side tagging (introduced_in_diff: bool on every finding)
scope_contract: --changed-files mode: inspector scans ONLY the listed changed files. Scope is cleanliness-of-THIS-diff only. Pre-existing historical violations in untouched files are not inspector scope. Default-safe rule: absent introduced_in_diff: true, close.md treats findings as pre-existing/non-blocking.

domain_check.rule: Prompt-inspector detection rules apply ONLY to markdown command/agent documentation: commands/*.md and agents/*.md files. Detection patterns (## Philosophy, ## Overview, ## Quality Standards, ## Safety Features, ## Helper Scripts, ## Usage) are markdown section headers.
domain_check.result: NONE of the 4 changed files are .md command/agent documentation files.

per_file_analysis.reason: Shell script infrastructure file — not a command/agent markdown doc. Contains inline code comments explaining safety rules, which are substantive procedural annotations, not verbose documentation sections subject to 'rules not stories' principle.
Shell script infrastructure file — not a command/agent markdown doc. Header comments describe the hook's step-by-step protocol, which are functional implementation notes, not explanatory fluff.
JSON policy configuration file — not a command/agent markdown doc. The '_note' field is a JSON string property documenting policy versioning history. No markdown section headers exist in JSON files.
JSON settings configuration file — not a command/agent markdown doc. Contains harness configuration keys/values only. No markdown section headers.

summary.verdict: CLEAN — no verbosity violations in this diff. All 4 changed files are outside the commands/*.md / agents/*.md verbosity scope (shell scripts and JSON config). No introduced_in_diff: true findings exist; close is not blocked by this inspector.
summary.notes: task 20260521-090200 changes are infrastructure-only (hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh M2/M3 sentinel alignment, hooks/userprompt-consent-allowlist.sh S1 regex-skip fix, policies/tool-policy.v1.json policy_version 4 codex path additions, settings.json allowlist updates). None constitute prompt/command documentation. Status 'not_applicable' would also be accurate; 'CLEAN' is used here to align with prior cycle convention for out-of-scope non-doc diffs (see prompt-inspector-report-20260522-000000.json).

new_vs_preexisting_mechanism: Mechanism (ii) — inspector-side tagging. In --changed-files mode, this inspector emits findings for the listed files tagged with introduced_in_diff: bool. Zero findings were emitted; the introduced_in_diff field is therefore not applicable. Absent a positive introduced_in_diff: true marker, close.md treats any finding as pre-existing per AC-2.6 (b) default-safe ignore rule.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed ZERO findings is correct. All 4 changed files are shell/JSON infrastructure — not commands/*.md or agents/*.md — and contain no verbose markdown sections. One improvement proposed: use clearer summary field names to distinguish 'files scanned' from 'files in verbosity scope'.

findings.issue: files_inspected=4 is ambiguous — in-domain prompt docs actually inspected is zero; the number 4 counts files scanned regardless of domain.
findings.proposed_fix: Use distinct summary fields: changed_files_scanned=4, files_in_verbosity_scope=0, files_out_of_verbosity_scope=4 instead of a single files_inspected=4.
findings.rationale: Applied. The summary now uses changed_files_scanned, files_in_verbosity_scope, and files_out_of_verbosity_scope to make the domain distinction explicit. This aligns with the precedent in prompt-inspector-report-20260522-000000.json which used the same field names.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied codex PROPOSED_FIX: summary uses changed_files_scanned/files_in_verbosity_scope/files_out_of_verbosity_scope instead of flat files_inspected.
